[2018-03-23 03:07] gadget246: if they don't buy blastforge early which one do you go for?
[2018-03-23 03:09] mqp: amporilla is irrelevant in base + amporilla + grimbotch
[2018-03-23 03:09] mqp: with or without, you play with grimbotch tarsier
[2018-03-23 03:10] gadget246: is it always grim+tarsier or do you ever go grim+grim?
[2018-03-23 03:11] .bky_1556 (bky): You want some mix of grim + tarsier.
[2018-03-23 03:11] .bky_1556 (bky): You do grim+grim whenever it's particularly inconvenient to defend, or when it's late.
[2018-03-23 03:12] .bky_1556 (bky): But you also want some permanent attack on the board when your botches time out.
[2018-03-23 03:12] gadget246: is killing an engineer next turn worth getting a grim instead of a tarsier? or would you need more incentive than that
[2018-03-23 03:14] .bky_1556 (bky): Depends on how late it is.
[2018-03-23 03:14] .bky_1556 (bky): But a good example of when you want a double botch turn early is where it forces the opponent to block with a drone or soak with an unclicked Rhino.
[2018-03-23 03:15] .bky_1556 (bky): It's something you just gotta learn to feel.
[2018-03-23 03:15] gadget246: how about base+grim+amp + that red unit that constructs husks
[2018-03-23 03:17] .bky_1556 (bky): I don't know. Corpus pushes the Botch-Tarsier mix a bit more towards Tarsier.
[2018-03-23 03:18] .bky_1556 (bky): I don't know if that's enough to enable Amporilla vs. tight play.
[2018-03-23 03:21] gadget246: I just played a game with those units as p2 where i bought out grimbotches and my opponent defended with R,W and corpus
we ended up with the same number of tarsiers and same-ish econ when grimbotches started running out, but his amporilla came online a half-turn sooner so I lost
and I'm trying to figure out if I lost to macro, micro, or p2 disadvantage in a red set

[2018-03-23 03:31] .bky_1556 (bky): First read is that you get tatsu yourself before odin hits.

[2018-03-23 03:32] xyotsuba: there is nothing to use for defense in a tatsu rush except wall and husk

[2018-03-23 21:11] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): I'm so not sure what to do with this set
[2018-03-23 21:11] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): I guess you go natural conduit?
[2018-03-23 21:11] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): but then what do next
[2018-03-23 21:11] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): this is clearly not a zemora set
[2018-03-23 21:11] supergreyman (Greyman): Why don't you think so?
[2018-03-23 21:12] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): stuff like cynestra, arms race, and colossus all seem like things that would really make it hard to survive while getting the greens you want
[2018-03-23 21:13] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): but maybe tantalum into zemora and/or fabricator is nice
[2018-03-23 21:13] supergreyman (Greyman): I personally would worry more about the tantalum, but yes, I wouldn't go green in this set either.
[2018-03-23 21:14] supergreyman (Greyman): Since the only good blocker is expensive.
[2018-03-23 21:14] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): yeah, and since everything else is so high-cost
[2018-03-23 21:14] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): the blocker isn't going to get to do a lot of absorbing
[2018-03-23 21:14] darkeisbein: prolly just tants > arms race?
[2018-03-23 21:14] supergreyman (Greyman): I think the Fabricator would be good here.
[2018-03-23 21:15] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, I think tantalum is more efficient.
[2018-03-23 21:15] supergreyman (Greyman): I would go at least green and blue in this set.
[2018-03-23 21:16] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): yeah, that looks like it's probably the best
[2018-03-23 21:16] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): get arms race if they go zemora but meh
[2018-03-23 21:16] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): auride core is nice here too
[2018-03-23 21:17] iminabearsuit: Collonkus seem good.
[2018-03-23 21:18] .bky_1556 (bky): You're getting a blastforge because wall tantalum > breechproof.
[2018-03-23 21:18] .bky_1556 (bky): The main questions are Omega and Colossus.
[2018-03-23 21:20] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): I died mostly to stupidity
[2018-03-23 21:21] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): like I forgot to build drones on turn 2
[2018-03-23 21:21] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): so it was kinda just catch-up all game anyways
[2018-03-23 21:22] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): also I think I went tantalums too fast
[2018-03-23 21:23] extratricky: I think p2's plan changes significantly depending on what p1 does for that set
[2018-03-23 21:23] .bky_1556 (bky): If you forgot to build drones on turn 2 that means you need earlier tantalums because you're sure not winning the long game.
[2018-03-23 21:23] .bky_1556 (bky): Unless by that you mean you skipped fabricator to get tantalums out.
[2018-03-23 21:25] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): I did skip fabricator

--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-03-24) ---
[2018-03-24 05:33] old.janx.spirit (oldjanxspirit): I'm a noob so I'm sorry if this is a  silly question, but in the opening moves of the game is it better to invest in attack or defense resources? I keep finding myself being outbuilt
[2018-03-24 05:35] namington: well, in the first handful of turns, its better to go for drones 😛 but beyond the early game, you should be generally building attack unless you *need* to build defense
[2018-03-24 05:36] namington: think about it like this: defense that isnt blocking anything is useless - it just sits there and does nothing
[2018-03-24 05:36] namington: meanwhile, attack forces your opponent to build stuff to block it
[2018-03-24 05:36] namington: in a way, attack hurts your opponent's resources, whereas preemptive defense doesn't
[2018-03-24 05:36] namington: it helps that most defense in the game is prompt, so (usually) you can put off building it until you *need* it
[2018-03-24 05:37] namington: and you generally dont want to build much more than is necessary to avoid a breach
[2018-03-24 05:37] amalloy: a tarsier kills one engineer per turn forever, whereas an engineer dies once and that's it
[2018-03-24 05:37] namington: there are a couple units that are exceptions, though - for example, preemptive infusion grid is good
[2018-03-24 05:37] namington: because infusion grid is crazy efficient (compare it to wall!), but not prompt
[2018-03-24 05:37] namington: in general, though, build attack unless you NEED to build defense
[2018-03-24 05:38] namington: and dont build more defense than you actually need
[2018-03-24 05:39] namington: for more on that sort of thing, I'd recommend reading through 307th's blog, and in particular this article: https://prismatalibrary.blog/2017/12/22/the-standard-style/
The Standard Style
This guide will teach you the standard style of play – a style of play that is simple enough for a beginner to be able to learn, while still being grounded in real high level Prismata strateg…
[2018-03-24 05:40] namington: point #2 being the relevant one to this discussion
[2018-03-24 05:40] .bky_1556 (bky): Absorbing - using all but one health on a non-fragile blocker - is really good early. You can nullify more attackers than the blocker's worth. But you only get one absorber.
[2018-03-24 05:40] namington: yeah, you generally want to build a single wall or something (even a rhino) when your opponent starts actually putting out damage
[2018-03-24 05:41] namington: basically, get the highest absorber you are able to
[2018-03-24 05:41] namington: until you NEED to build defense
[2018-03-24 05:41] .bky_1556 (bky): Conversely, you want to wait to start pumping out attackers until you can, within a couple of turns, produce more attack than your opponent can absorb.
[2018-03-24 05:41] namington: ~~or just go with the lifecoach strategy of breachproof-every-game~~
[2018-03-24 05:42] amalloy: i feel like this would be information overload for someone who asked if defenders are better than attackers
[2018-03-24 05:42] namington: its a difficult question though cause
[2018-03-24 05:42] .bky_1556 (bky): (One attacker, to force them to actually build the absorber, can be okay)
[2018-03-24 05:42] namington: the balacne between attack, defense, and eocnomy is the very foundation of prismata
[2018-03-24 05:42] namington: like as soon as that balance is broken, one player wins.
[2018-03-24 05:43] namington: so naturally even a "simple" quesiton like that has a complex answer - I see what you mean though
[2018-03-24 05:43] .bky_1556 (bky): So the full but simplified sequence for a standard strategy goes like this:
[2018-03-24 05:43] namington: also I cant type x_x
[2018-03-24 05:44] .bky_1556 (bky): Drones first, then an absorbing defender and a few more drones, then attackers, then more blockers as you need them & attackers otherwise.
[2018-03-24 05:45] namington: wait I thought every game was 1. DD 2. DD 3. DDA 4. A 5. Lucina???? :LongNoseLiar:
[2018-03-24 05:46] .bky_1556 (bky): There's an occasional game where Lucina isn't in the set.
[2018-03-24 05:46] namington: lucina for base set when
[2018-03-24 05:46] namington: "in order to balance BSO, we decided to implement a way for P1 to counter P2's DD into DA opening. Therefore, blastforge has been replaced in the base set by lucina."
[2018-03-24 05:47] Deleted User (Deleted User): cutting drones :DansGame:
[2018-03-24 05:47] f300xen (f0x): Can't cut drones if you can't buy them
[2018-03-24 05:48] namington: objectively the best opening strategy
[2018-03-24 05:49] namington: actually, now that I look at that image: How does Doomed Drone being in the set impact how often you go for Savior?
[2018-03-24 05:49] namington: ie,are there that many sets where normally you'd go for savior, but if doomed drone is added, you don't? or vice versa
[2018-03-24 05:50] namington: I'd *imagine* doomed drone makes savior a bit less good but I dont have enough experience with those sets honestly
[2018-03-24 05:50] .bky_1556 (bky): I would think base+8+doomed drone reduces the probability of savior over base+8.
[2018-03-24 05:50] Deleted User (Deleted User): it supports both the savior build and the attacking the savior build
[2018-03-24 05:50] namington: thats where it feels weird
[2018-03-24 05:50] namington: doomed drone supports *everything*
[2018-03-24 05:50] namington: so I'm not sure which strategy it supports *more*
[2018-03-24 05:50] mqp: i prefer it for the savior side
[2018-03-24 05:50] .bky_1556 (bky): I think it supports the non-savior build more.
[2018-03-24 05:50] f300xen (f0x): Doomed drone reduces the probability of savior unless it doesn't
[2018-03-24 05:51] mqp: the savior side needs soak and already wants to have extra energy
[2018-03-24 05:51] mqp: the savior side also may not be doing damage early enough for the non-savior side's early doomed drones to get value
[2018-03-24 05:55] Deleted User (Deleted User): here's the interesting pros for both

--- Thread (14 messages, 2018-03-24) ---
[2018-03-24 13:51] jonshea: Hi friends. I’ve been playing for a couple days and can usually (but not always) beat Adept bot. Say I’m p1, I open with a third engineer on turn 2 (because there are big blockers in the random set) and my opponent  responds with a tech building. How do I respond to that? Do I build 3 drones and if they drop an attacker I tank it with the engineer or a drone? Or do I cut drones to build my own tech so that the next turn I can maybe cut drones to build a prompt defender?
[2018-03-24 13:54] crash_overlord: Tank on the engineers. If theres actual big absorb in the set you need to get the drones to be able to drop it
[2018-03-24 13:54] wilson0862 (Wilson): Only build the third engi if you are going to use all the energy on t3.
[2018-03-24 13:54] crash_overlord: if you drop tech building you're comitting yourself to a lower econ and ended up wasting gold on an engineer
[2018-03-24 13:54] jonshea: But if I get attacked I’m stuck on lower econ and wasted gold too?
[2018-03-24 13:55] crash_overlord: I mean you let your opponent attack you for 1 turn
[2018-03-24 13:55] wilson0862 (Wilson): Why do you think that is the case?
[2018-03-24 13:55] crash_overlord: 1 turn where you have no absorb*
[2018-03-24 13:56] crash_overlord: So say turn 2 you drop engineer, your opponent responds with tech building, you buy 3 drones, your opponent buys damage. Now you can buy 3 drones and tech building > buying defense on your opponents next attack.
[2018-03-24 13:57] jonshea: If I block with the engineer then I'm back to 2 engineers, I've netted one drone at the cost of 2 extra gold? If I block with a drone that hardly seems better?
[2018-03-24 13:57] crash_overlord: Why would you block with drone 🤔
[2018-03-24 13:58] awaclus: you've netted one drone and blocked one attack at the cost of 2 extra gold
[2018-03-24 13:59] awaclus: blocking one attack for 2 gold is fine
[2018-03-24 13:59] jonshea: Alright. Thanks everyone.

[2018-03-25 05:20] namington: let's say there's a hypothetical set which is obviously breachproof (has bp economy + a good bp attacker, not many other options, etc), but contains both venge cannons and another bp drone sink unit (trinity, dynamo)

[2018-03-25 08:30] mtanzer: let me try to explain it without inflation.  strat A: hold arka turn 1 and turn 2.  strat B: click arka turn 1, hold turn 2.  in strat B we buy 7 more soak (think like wall + doomed wall) this turn, and get 4 damage on turn 1.  in strat A we buy 7 soak worth of attack turn 1 (think 2 splitters) and deal 4 less damage.  on turn 1 strat A got 4 more absorb, but bought 7 less soak, so A has to buy 3 more soak on t2 compared to B, so B gets to buy 3 soak worth of attack instead (let's just estimate this as 1 splitter).  so at the end of t2, B got an arka swing for 4 damage but has 1 less splitter, and A still got 2 damage in from the splitter attacks.  so A is up a splitter and down 2 damage.
[2018-03-25 08:30] mtanzer: the idea is 4 tempo is worth roughly 1 damage, because instead of buying 4 soak you can buy 1 attacker a turn sooner, and that attacker gets to attack 1 extra time
[2018-03-25 08:31] mtanzer: since arka is 7 tempo, the mistake of clicking it costs us roughly 2 attack.  a splitter is worth about 4 attack, which is 2 attack better than just having an extra 2 damage

[2018-03-25 22:25] mrguy888: Holding Omega gives you 6 hp on the very turn you are holding Omega. It is pretty clear that you don't have buy 2 walls that you would have to buy if you clicked Omega. It isn't your opponent's turn so they obviously are unaffected by whether you click or not at this point in time.

After you pass your turn your opponent is affected by your choice. If you clicked Omega they have to buy 1 additional Wall. 1 is clearly less than 2. 

Assuming you understand that buying defence that you don't need is a bad play because Walls don't produce resources, it is pretty clear that holding Omega is way better than clicking it with Wall absorb.

Player 1 starts with 6 drones, P2 starts with 7. This is balanced because p1 gets their stuff first. This points to the fact that even if the trade is 1 wall for 1 wall, which it isn't, then it is still a bad trade for you because you have to do the bad play first.
